title: Reform to the EU customs union agreed
content: Parliament and Council have agreed on the most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968. The new rules introduce new measures for e-commerce and modern, data-driven customs architecture that simplifies procedures and enhances efficiency.
snippet: Parliament and Council have agreed on the most ambitious reform of EU customs rules since 1968. The new rules introduce new measures for e-commerce and modern, data-driven customs architecture that si

title: New rules to address bank failures protecting taxpayers and depositors
content: New rules will help better protect taxpayers and depositors by broadening the scope of banks covered by the EU’s laws on bank failure. They also empower authorities to manage potential bank failures more effectively and harmonise depositor protection across the EU.

title: EU to modernise how it tackles rising wildfire threat
content: The Commission has put forward a new strategy for wildfire risk management that covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery. 2025 was the worst wildfire season in Europe since records began. This strategy will help enhance resilience to wildfires and protect people and the environment.
snippet: The Commission has put forward a new strategy for wildfire risk management that covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery. 2025 was the worst wildfire season in Europe since records began

title: EU and Australia strengthen ties with trade agreement
content: The EU and Australia have concluded negotiations on a free trade agreement. It will remove over 99% of tariffs on EU exports to Australia, improve access to critical raw materials, and strengthen strategic ties with the Indo-Pacific. They also signed a Security and Defence Partnership.

title: EU grants €2.7 billion to clean industry projects
content: Some 54 clean industry projects will receive €2.7 billion of funding from the EU’s Innovation Fund. The projects span 17 countries and will support innovative, decarbonisation solutions such as refineries, cement and lime, renewable energy, as well as maritime, aviation and road transport.

title: EU road deaths drop by 3% in 2025
content: New figures show 3% fewer people died on European roads in 2025 compared to 2024. Some 19,400 road fatalities were reported in 2025, 580 fewer than the previous year. However, most EU countries are not yet on track to meet the EU's goal of halving road deaths and serious injuries by 2030.

title: EU funds literary projects for translation and publication
content: Creative Europe will fund the translation of nearly 500 works of fiction from 36 into 24 languages. In the next three years, the works, mostly youth literature and novels, will be made available to readers across Europe and beyond, showcasing the rich linguistic diversity of European literature.

title: Remembering the Brussels terror attacks 10 years later
content: This week marks 10 years since the terrorist attacks in Brussels in 2016. As commemorations take place to remember the victims and survivors, we take look at what the EU is doing to fight terrorism and extremism and to boost security.
snippet: This week marks 10 years since the terrorist attacks in Brussels in 2016. As commemorations take place to remember the victims and survivors, we take look at what the EU is doing to fight terrorism an

title: EU leaders launch 'One Europe, One Market' agenda
content: At the EU summit, leaders agreed on concrete measures and deadlines to strengthen EU competitiveness. They also addressed the situation in Iran and energy security, the EU's long-term budget, Ukraine and defence.
snippet: At the EU summit, leaders agreed on concrete measures and deadlines to strengthen EU competitiveness. They also addressed the situation in Iran and energy security, the EU's long-term budget, Ukraine

title: EU Inc.:making business easier in the European Union
content: The European Commission has presented EU Inc., a new single set of corporate rules that will be the same and apply across the EU. This will make it easier for businesses to start, operate and grow across the EU. Its optional, fully digital procedures will enable innovative companies to scale up.
snippet: The European Commission has presented EU Inc., a new single set of corporate rules that will be the same and apply across the EU. This will make it easier for businesses to start, operate and grow acr

title: Middle East: EU announces €458 million in humanitarian aid
content: The EU is supplying €458 million in humanitarian aid for Palestine, Lebanon, Syria, Jordan and Egypt in 2026. The aid mostly comprises food assistance, health, protection, shelter and education. With many other donors withdrawing from the region, the EU is sustaining life-saving aid to millions.
snippet: The EU is supplying €458 million in humanitarian aid for Palestine, Lebanon, Syria, Jordan and Egypt in 2026. The aid mostly comprises food assistance, health, protection, shelter and education. With

title: Securing clean and affordable energy for EU citizens and companies
content: The Commission has presented steps to boost investment in homegrown clean energy solutions, increase resilience, and reduce energy prices. Speeding up the shift to clean energy is essential to secure Europe’s strategic independence, competitiveness, and long-term sustainable growth.
snippet: The Commission has presented steps to boost investment in homegrown clean energy solutions, increase resilience, and reduce energy prices. Speeding up the shift to clean energy is essential to secure

title: Middle East: EU steps up efforts to bring citizens home
content: Two repatriation flights chartered by the European Commission have brought back 356 European citizens who were stranded in the Middle East from Oman to Romania. So far, the EU has supported 42 flights, bringing over 4,100 European citizens safely back to EU countries. More flights are planned.
snippet: Two repatriation flights chartered by the European Commission have brought back 356 European citizens who were stranded in the Middle East from Oman to Romania. So far, the EU has supported 42 flights

title: EU invests over €103 million in environment and climate projects
content: Projects in seven EU countries will receive EU funding worth more than €103 million to improve public health and quality of life across the continent. They focus notably on climate and water resilience, nature restoration, circular economy and sustainable land use.
snippet: Projects in seven EU countries will receive EU funding worth more than €103 million to improve public health and quality of life across the continent. They focus notably on climate and water resilienc

title: The EU in 2025: a year of clarity and resolve
content: In a year where the world became more volatile and fragmented, the EU assumed more responsibility for its own security and stepped up efforts to boost its defence. It also continued to deliver for citizens and businesses. Find out more from the General report on the activities of the EU in 2025.
snippet: In a year where the world became more volatile and fragmented, the EU assumed more responsibility for its own security and stepped up efforts to boost its defence. It also continued to deliver for cit

title: Deal on measures to reinforce farmers’ position in the food supply chain
content: A deal has been struck on new measures that will help stabilise farmers’ income. The agreement will help set fair prices for farmers’ products; establish clearer labelling and advertising rules and for the first time, stricter labelling rules for some meat products.
snippet: A deal has been struck on new measures that will help stabilise farmers’ income. The agreement will help set fair prices for farmers’ products; establish clearer labelling and advertising rules and fo

title: New strategy embeds gender equality into every aspect of life
content: Ahead of International Women's Day on 8 March, the European Commission has presented its gender equality strategy for 2026-2030. It sets out concrete actions to empower women and girls, engage men and boys, oppose any backtracking on fundamental rights, and accelerate progress.
snippet: Ahead of International Women's Day on 8 March, the European Commission has presented its gender equality strategy for 2026-2030. It sets out concrete actions to empower women and girls, engage men and

title: EU releases first data on minimum wages in 2026
content: According to the first EU data on minimum wages for 2026, minimum wages in the EU ranged on 1 January from €620 per month in Bulgaria to €2 704 in Luxembourg. Find out more about why the minimum wage is key and why wages don’t tell the full story when it comes to local spending power.
snippet: According to the first EU data on minimum wages for 2026, minimum wages in the EU ranged on 1 January from €620 per month in Bulgaria to €2 704 in Luxembourg. Find out more about why the minimum wage

title: EU acts against dangerous products and stronger consumer protection
content: Safety Gate, the European rapid alert system for dangerous non-food products, recorded the highest number of alerts (4,671) last year since its launch in 2003. The most frequently reported dangerous products were cosmetics (36%), toys (16%) and electrical appliances and equipment (11%).
snippet: Safety Gate, the European rapid alert system for dangerous non-food products, recorded the highest number of alerts (4,671) last year since its launch in 2003. The most frequently reported dangerous p

title: Exposure to pollution increases risk of mental health issues
content: A new EU report finds a significant link between exposure to pollution (air, noise and chemicals) and mental health issues like depression and anxiety. The report gives the latest scientific findings on the topic and offers courses of action, like the positive effects of nature on mental wellbeing.
snippet: A new EU report finds a significant link between exposure to pollution (air, noise and chemicals) and mental health issues like depression and anxiety. The report gives the latest scientific findings

title: EU and Switzerland sign agreements to deepen and expand their relationship
content: The EU has signed a package of agreements with Switzerland that will help deepen and expand their mutual relationship. It enables frictionless access to a market of 460 million consumers in key sectors, delivering economic benefits to both sides.
snippet: The EU has signed a package of agreements with Switzerland that will help deepen and expand their mutual relationship. It enables frictionless access to a market of 460 million consumers in key sector

title: EU statement on developments in the Middle East
content: The European Union is following developments in Iran and the Middle East with utmost concern.
snippet: The European Union is following developments in Iran and the Middle East with utmost concern.

title: EU takes aim at trafficking of illicit firearms
content: Illicit firearms are a threat to public safety, fuelling terrorism, organised crime, and other offences. The Commission has proposed new EU-wide rules to tackle trafficking and other firearms-related offences. These will make investigations and prosecutions more effective and help law enforcement.
snippet: Illicit firearms are a threat to public safety, fuelling terrorism, organised crime, and other offences. The Commission has proposed new EU-wide rules to tackle trafficking and other firearms-related

title: Four years on: Europe stands with Ukraine
content: On February 24 2022, Russia launched an unprovoked war of aggression against Ukraine. Since then Ukrainians have endured years of unnecessary suffering. The EU has provided unwavering political, financial, military and humanitarian aid since the outset and reaffirms its support to Ukraine today.
snippet: On February 24 2022, Russia launched an unprovoked war of aggression against Ukraine. Since then Ukrainians have endured years of unnecessary suffering. The EU has provided unwavering political, finan

title: Infant formula: low risk of contamination due to recalls
content: The EU's health and food safety agencies say the risk to exposure to contaminated infant formula is now low, thanks to the recall in several EU countries. Most of the affected children experience mild symptoms. The recall was triggered in December after a toxin called cereulide was detected.
snippet: The EU's health and food safety agencies say the risk to exposure to contaminated infant formula is now low, thanks to the recall in several EU countries. Most of the affected children experience mild

title: Two years of ensuring safer online spaces
content: Two years ago, the Digital Services Act entered into force. It regulates online services such as social media, marketplaces, app stores, and online travel and accommodation services. Its objective is to ensure a safer digital space in which fundamental rights are protected.
snippet: Two years ago, the Digital Services Act entered into force. It regulates online services such as social media, marketplaces, app stores, and online travel and accommodation services. Its objective is

title: European Citizens’ Initiative – 15 years and going strong!
content: This year we celebrate 15 years of the European Citizens’ Initiative - a unique way for you to act on causes you care about. Launch an initiative that falls within the EU’s competences and shape the EU by calling on the European Commission to propose new laws on your chosen issue. Find out more.

title: EU leaders push for a more competitive and resilient Europe
content: At the informal retreat, EU leaders committed to making Europe strategically autonomous. The ultimate goal of these efforts is to pursue economic growth and industrial innovation and deliver high-quality jobs and affordability.
